explanations.note: call meanings follow standard 2/1 Game Force

options.text: 3♣ — inviting — 7-9 HCP, 5+ ♣. Next call is usually Pass (69%); 3♥ (9%); 3♠ (8%). A strong engine chooses this 43% of the time here. Where it leads: 3CE (27%), 4CE (13%), 5CXE (7%). The winner (EV gap >= 0.5 IMPs): +0.5 IMPs vs Pass (±0.6); wins on 39% of layouts against 43%.

Pass — limited — at most 9 HCP. Next call is usually 1♥ (28%); 1♠ (21%); 2♥ (17%). A strong engine chooses this 32% of the time here. Where it leads: 4HN (19%), 4SN (18%), 3HN (8%). Scored -0.5 IMPs vs the top choice (±0.6); wins on 43% of layouts, pushes on 19%.

2♣ — preemptive — 3-6 HCP, 5+ ♣. Next call is usually Pass (32%); Double (12%); 2♥ (12%). A strong engine chooses this 20% of the time here. Where it leads: 4SN (11%), 4HN (11%), 5CXE (7%). Scored -1.0 IMPs vs the top choice (±0.6); wins on 32% of layouts, pushes on 29%.

4♣ — preemptive — 3-6 HCP, 6+ ♣. Next call is usually Pass (71%); Double (22%); 4♥ (4%); most common continuation: Pass Pass Pass (36%). A strong engine chooses this 14% of the time here. Where it leads: 4CE (36%), 4CXE (29%), 5CXE (9%). Scored -2.8 IMPs vs the top choice (±0.6); wins on 22% of layouts, pushes on 19%.

classification.type_reason: Hero's weak shapely hand with long clubs faces a decision on how high to compete obstructively (2C/3C/4C) rather than whether to just bid or pass a settled contract.
